rules.name: builtin-variable-shadowing
rules.description: Variable {name} is shadowing a Python builtin

rules.name: builtin-argument-shadowing
rules.description: Function argument {name} is shadowing a Python builtin

rules.name: builtin-attribute-shadowing
rules.description: Python builtin is shadowed by class attribute {name} from {row}

rules.name: builtin-import-shadowing
rules.description: Import {name} is shadowing a Python builtin
rules.exampleBad:
from rich import print

print("Some message")
rules.exampleGood:
from rich import print as rich_print

rich_print("Some message")

rules.name: stdlib-module-shadowing
rules.description: Module {name} shadows a Python standard-library module
rules.exampleBad:
$ touch random.py
$ python3 -c 'from random import choice'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
    from random import choice
ImportError: cannot import name 'choice' from 'random' (consider renaming '/random.py' since it has the same name as the standard library module named 'random' and prevents importing that standard library module)

rules.name: builtin-lambda-argument-shadowing
rules.description: Lambda argument {name} is shadowing a Python builtin

rules.name: airflow-variable-name-task-id-mismatch
rules.description: Task variable name should match the task_id : "{task_id}"
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.operators import PythonOperator


incorrect_name = PythonOperator(task_id="my_task")
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.operators import PythonOperator


my_task = PythonOperator(task_id="my_task")

rules.name: airflow-dag-no-schedule-argument
rules.description: DAG or @dag should have an explicit schedule argument
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow import DAG


# Using the implicit default schedule.
dag = DAG(dag_id="my_dag")
rules.exampleGood:
from datetime import timedelta

from airflow import DAG


dag = DAG(dag_id="my_dag", schedule=timedelta(days=1))

rules.name: airflow-variable-get-outside-task
rules.description: Variable.get() outside of a task
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.sdk import Variable
from airflow.operators.bash import BashOperator


foo = Variable.get("foo")
BashOperator(task_id="bad", bash_command="echo $FOO", env={"FOO": foo})
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.operators.bash import BashOperator


BashOperator(
    task_id="good",
    bash_command="echo $FOO",
    env={"FOO": "{{ var.value.foo }}"},
)

rules.name: airflow-task-branch-as-short-circuit
rules.description: @task.branch can be replaced with @task.short_circuit

rules.name: airflow-xcom-pull-in-template-string
rules.description: Use the .output attribute on the task object for "{task_id}" instead of xcom_pull in a template string
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator


task_1 = PythonOperator(task_id="task_1", python_callable=my_func)
task_2 = PythonOperator(
    task_id="task_2",
    op_args="{{ ti.xcom_pull(task_ids='task_1') }}",
)
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator


task_1 = PythonOperator(task_id="task_1", python_callable=my_func)
task_2 = PythonOperator(
    task_id="task_2",
    op_args=task_1.output,
)

rules.name: airflow-task-implicit-multiple-outputs
rules.description: @task -decorated function relies on multiple_outputs inference

rules.name: airflow3-removal
rules.description: {deprecated} is removed in Airflow 3.0
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.utils.dates import days_ago


yesterday = days_ago(today, 1)
rules.exampleGood:
from datetime import timedelta


yesterday = today - timedelta(days=1)

rules.name: airflow3-moved-to-provider
rules.description: {deprecated} is moved into {provider} provider in Airflow 3.0;
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.auth.managers.fab.fab_auth_manager import FabAuthManager

fab_auth_manager_app = FabAuthManager().get_fastapi_app()
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.providers.fab.auth_manager.fab_auth_manager import FabAuthManager

fab_auth_manager_app = FabAuthManager().get_fastapi_app()

rules.name: airflow3-incompatible-function-signature
rules.description: {function_name} signature is changed in Airflow 3.0
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.lineage.hook import HookLineageCollector

collector = HookLineageCollector()
# Passing positional arguments will raise a runtime error in Airflow 3.0
collector.create_asset("s3://bucket/key")
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.lineage.hook import HookLineageCollector

collector = HookLineageCollector()
# Passing arguments as keyword arguments instead of positional arguments
collector.create_asset(uri="s3://bucket/key")

rules.name: airflow3-dag-dynamic-value
rules.description: {function_name}() produces a value that changes at runtime; using it in a Dag or task argument causes infinite Dag version creation
rules.exampleBad:
from datetime import datetime

from airflow import DAG

dag = DAG(dag_id="my_dag", start_date=datetime.now())
rules.exampleGood:
from datetime import datetime

from airflow import DAG

dag = DAG(dag_id="my_dag", start_date=datetime(2024, 1, 1))

rules.name: airflow3-suggested-update
rules.description: {deprecated} is removed in Airflow 3.0; It still works in Airflow 3.0 but is expected to be removed in a future version.
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow import Dataset


Dataset(uri="test://test/")
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.sdk import Asset


Asset(uri="test://test/")

rules.name: airflow3-suggested-to-move-to-provider
rules.description: {deprecated} is deprecated and moved into {provider} provider in Airflow 3.0; It still works in Airflow 3.0 but is expected to be removed in a future version.
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator


def print_context(ds=None, **kwargs):
    print(kwargs)
    print(ds)


print_the_context = PythonOperator(
    task_id="print_the_context", python_callable=print_context
)
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.providers.standard.operators.python import PythonOperator


def print_context(ds=None, **kwargs):
    print(kwargs)
    print(ds)


print_the_context = PythonOperator(
    task_id="print_the_context", python_callable=print_context
)

rules.name: airflow31-moved
rules.description: {deprecated} is moved in Airflow 3.1
rules.exampleBad:
from airflow.utils.timezone import convert_to_utc
from datetime import datetime

convert_to_utc(datetime.now())
rules.exampleGood:
from airflow.sdk.timezone import convert_to_utc
from datetime import datetime

convert_to_utc(datetime.now())

rules.name: missing-type-function-argument
rules.description: Missing type annotation for function argument {name}
rules.exampleBad: def foo(x): ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(x: int): ...

rules.name: missing-type-args
rules.description: Missing type annotation for *{name}
rules.exampleBad: def foo(*args): ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(*args: int): ...

rules.name: missing-type-kwargs
rules.description: Missing type annotation for **{name}
rules.exampleBad: def foo(**kwargs): ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(**kwargs: int): ...

rules.name: missing-return-type-undocumented-public-function
rules.description: Missing return type annotation for public function {name}
rules.exampleBad:
def add(a, b):
    return a + b
rules.exampleGood:
def add(a: int, b: int) -> int:
    return a + b

rules.name: missing-return-type-private-function
rules.description: Missing return type annotation for private function {name}
rules.exampleBad:
def _add(a, b):
    return a + b
rules.exampleGood:
def _add(a: int, b: int) -> int:
    return a + b

rules.name: missing-return-type-special-method
rules.description: Missing return type annotation for special method {name}
rules.exampleBad:
[tool.ruff.lint.flake8-annotations]
mypy-init-return = true

rules.name: missing-return-type-static-method
rules.description: Missing return type annotation for staticmethod {name}

rules.name: missing-return-type-class-method
rules.description: Missing return type annotation for classmethod {name}

rules.name: any-type
rules.description: Dynamically typed expressions (typing.Any) are disallowed in {name}
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Any


def foo(x: Any): ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(x: int): ...

rules.name: unused-function-argument
rules.description: Unused function argument: {name}
rules.exampleBad:
def foo(bar, baz):
    return bar * 2
rules.exampleGood:
def foo(bar):
    return bar * 2

rules.name: unused-method-argument
rules.description: Unused method argument: {name}

rules.name: unused-class-method-argument
rules.description: Unused class method argument: {name}

rules.name: unused-static-method-argument
rules.description: Unused static method argument: {name}

rules.name: unused-lambda-argument
rules.description: Unused lambda argument: {name}
rules.exampleBad:
my_list = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squares = map(lambda x, y: x**2, my_list)
rules.exampleGood:
my_list = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squares = map(lambda x: x**2, my_list)

rules.name: cancel-scope-no-checkpoint
rules.description: A with {method_name}(...): context does not contain any await statements. This makes it pointless, as the timeout can only be triggered by a checkpoint.
rules.exampleBad:
import asyncio


async def func():
    async with asyncio.timeout(2):
        do_something()
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio


async def func():
    async with asyncio.timeout(2):
        do_something()
        await awaitable()

rules.name: trio-sync-call
rules.description: Call to {method_name} is not immediately awaited
rules.exampleBad:
import trio


async def double_sleep(x):
    trio.sleep(2 * x)
rules.exampleGood:
import trio


async def double_sleep(x):
    await trio.sleep(2 * x)

rules.name: async-function-with-timeout
rules.description: Async function definition with a timeout parameter
rules.exampleBad:
async def long_running_task(timeout): ...


async def main():
    await long_running_task(timeout=2)
rules.exampleGood:
async def long_running_task(): ...


async def main():
    async with asyncio.timeout(2):
        await long_running_task()

rules.name: async-busy-wait
rules.description: Use {module}.Event instead of awaiting {module}.sleep in a while loop
rules.exampleBad:
import asyncio

DONE = False


async def func():
    while not DONE:
        await asyncio.sleep(1)
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio

DONE = asyncio.Event()


async def func():
    await DONE.wait()

rules.name: async-zero-sleep
rules.description: Use {module}.lowlevel.checkpoint() instead of {module}.sleep(0)
rules.exampleBad:
import trio


async def func():
    await trio.sleep(0)
rules.exampleGood:
import trio.lowlevel


async def func():
    await trio.lowlevel.checkpoint()

rules.name: long-sleep-not-forever
rules.description: {module}.sleep() with >24 hour interval should usually be {module}.sleep_forever()
rules.exampleBad:
import trio


async def func():
    await trio.sleep(86401)
rules.exampleGood:
import trio


async def func():
    await trio.sleep_forever()

rules.name: yield-in-context-manager-in-async-generator
rules.description: Yield in context manager in async generator may not trigger cleanup
rules.exampleBad:
async def open_connection():
    async with connect() as conn:
        yield conn
rules.exampleGood:
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager


@asynccontextmanager
async def open_connection():
    async with connect() as conn:
        yield conn

rules.name: blocking-http-call-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not call blocking HTTP methods
rules.exampleBad:
import urllib


async def fetch():
    urllib.request.urlopen("https://example.com/foo/bar").read()
rules.exampleGood:
import aiohttp


async def fetch():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        async with session.get("https://example.com/foo/bar") as resp:
            ...

rules.name: blocking-http-call-httpx-in-async-function
rules.description: Blocking httpx method {name}.{call}() in async context, use httpx.AsyncClient
rules.exampleBad:
import httpx


async def fetch():
    client = httpx.Client()
    response = client.get(...)
rules.exampleGood:
import httpx


async def fetch():
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        response = await client.get(...)

rules.name: create-subprocess-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not create subprocesses with blocking methods
rules.exampleBad:
import os


async def foo():
    os.popen(cmd)
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio


async def foo():
    asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(cmd)

rules.name: run-process-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not run processes with blocking methods
rules.exampleBad:
import subprocess


async def foo():
    subprocess.run(cmd)
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio


async def foo():
    asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(cmd)

rules.name: wait-for-process-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not wait on processes with blocking methods
rules.exampleBad:
import os


async def foo():
    os.waitpid(0)
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio
import os


def wait_for_process():
    os.waitpid(0)


async def foo():
    await asyncio.loop.run_in_executor(None, wait_for_process)

rules.name: blocking-open-call-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not open files with blocking methods like open
rules.exampleBad:
async def foo():
    with open("bar.txt") as f:
        contents = f.read()
rules.exampleGood:
import anyio


async def foo():
    async with await anyio.open_file("bar.txt") as f:
        contents = await f.read()

rules.name: blocking-path-method-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not use {path_library} methods, use trio.Path or anyio.path
rules.exampleBad:
import os


async def func():
    path = "my_file.txt"
    file_exists = os.path.exists(path)
rules.exampleGood:
import trio


async def func():
    path = trio.Path("my_file.txt")
    file_exists = await path.exists()

rules.name: blocking-input-in-async-function
rules.description: Blocking call to input() in async context
rules.exampleBad:
async def foo():
    username = input("Username:")
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio


async def foo():
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    username = await loop.run_in_executor(None, input, "Username:")

rules.name: blocking-sleep-in-async-function
rules.description: Async functions should not call time.sleep
rules.exampleBad:
import time


async def fetch():
    time.sleep(1)
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio


async def fetch():
    await asyncio.sleep(1)

rules.name: unary-prefix-increment-decrement
rules.description: Python does not support the unary prefix increment operator ( ++ )

rules.name: assignment-to-os-environ
rules.description: Assigning to os.environ doesn't clear the environment
rules.exampleBad:
import os

os.environ = {"foo": "bar"}
rules.exampleGood:
import os

os.environ.clear()
os.environ["foo"] = "bar"

rules.name: unreliable-callable-check
rules.description: Using hasattr(x, "__call__") to test if x is callable is unreliable. Use callable(x) for consistent results.
rules.exampleBad: hasattr(obj, "__call__")
rules.exampleGood: callable(obj)

rules.name: strip-with-multi-characters
rules.description: Using .strip() with multi-character strings is misleading
rules.exampleBad: "text.txt".strip(".txt")  # "e"
rules.exampleGood: "text.txt".removesuffix(".txt")  # "text"

rules.name: mutable-argument-default
rules.description: Do not use mutable data structures for argument defaults
rules.exampleBad:
def add_to_list(item, some_list=[]):
    some_list.append(item)
    return some_list


l1 = add_to_list(0)  # [0]
l2 = add_to_list(1)  # [0, 1]
rules.exampleGood:
def add_to_list(item, some_list=None):
    if some_list is None:
        some_list = []
    some_list.append(item)
    return some_list


l1 = add_to_list(0)  # [0]
l2 = add_to_list(1)  # [1]

rules.name: unused-loop-control-variable
rules.description: Loop control variable {name} not used within loop body
rules.exampleBad:
for i, j in foo:
    bar(i)
rules.exampleGood:
for i, _j in foo:
    bar(i)

rules.name: function-call-in-default-argument
rules.description: Do not perform function call {name} in argument defaults; instead, perform the call within the function, or read the default from a module-level singleton variable

rules.name: get-attr-with-constant
rules.description: Do not call getattr with a constant attribute value. It is not any safer than normal property access.
rules.exampleBad: getattr(obj, "foo")
rules.exampleGood: obj.foo

rules.name: set-attr-with-constant
rules.description: Do not call setattr with a constant attribute value. It is not any safer than normal property access.
rules.exampleBad: setattr(obj, "foo", 42)
rules.exampleGood: obj.foo = 42

rules.name: assert-false
rules.description: Do not assert False ( python -O removes these calls), raise AssertionError()
rules.exampleBad: assert False
rules.exampleGood: raise AssertionError

rules.name: jump-statement-in-finally
rules.description: {name} inside finally blocks cause exceptions to be silenced

rules.name: redundant-tuple-in-exception-handler
rules.description: A length-one tuple literal is redundant in exception handlers

rules.name: duplicate-handler-exception
rules.description: Exception handler with duplicate exception: {name}
rules.exampleBad:
try:
    ...
except (Exception, ValueError):  # `Exception` includes `ValueError`.
    ...

rules.name: useless-comparison
rules.description: Pointless comparison. Did you mean to assign a value? Otherwise, prepend assert or remove it.
rules.exampleBad: foo == bar
rules.exampleGood: assert foo == bar, "`foo` and `bar` should be equal."

rules.name: raise-literal
rules.description: Cannot raise a literal. Did you intend to return it or raise an Exception?
rules.exampleBad: raise "foo"
rules.exampleGood: raise Exception("foo")

rules.name: assert-raises-exception
rules.description: Do not assert blind exception: {exception}
rules.exampleBad: self.assertRaises(Exception, foo)
rules.exampleGood: self.assertRaises(SomeSpecificException, foo)

rules.name: useless-expression
rules.description: Found useless expression. Either assign it to a variable or remove it.

rules.name: cached-instance-method
rules.description: Use of functools.lru_cache or functools.cache on methods can lead to memory leaks

rules.name: loop-variable-overrides-iterator
rules.description: Loop control variable {name} overrides iterable it iterates

rules.name: f-string-docstring
rules.description: f-string used as docstring. Python will interpret this as a joined string, rather than a docstring.
rules.exampleBad:
def foo():
    f"""Not a docstring."""
rules.exampleGood:
def foo():
    """A docstring."""

rules.name: useless-contextlib-suppress
rules.description: No arguments passed to contextlib.suppress . No exceptions will be suppressed and therefore this context manager is redundant
rules.exampleBad:
import contextlib

with contextlib.suppress():
    foo()
rules.exampleGood:
import contextlib

with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
    foo()

rules.name: function-uses-loop-variable
rules.description: Function definition does not bind loop variable {name}
rules.exampleBad:
adders = [lambda x: x + i for i in range(3)]
values = [adder(1) for adder in adders]  # [3, 3, 3]
rules.exampleGood:
adders = [lambda x, i=i: x + i for i in range(3)]
values = [adder(1) for adder in adders]  # [1, 2, 3]

rules.name: abstract-base-class-without-abstract-method
rules.description: {name} is an abstract base class, but it has no abstract methods or properties

rules.name: duplicate-try-block-exception
rules.description: try-except* block with duplicate exception {name}

rules.name: star-arg-unpacking-after-keyword-arg
rules.description: Star-arg unpacking after a keyword argument is strongly discouraged
rules.exampleBad:
def foo(x, y, z):
    return x, y, z


foo(1, 2, 3)  # (1, 2, 3)
foo(1, *[2, 3])  # (1, 2, 3)
# foo(x=1, *[2, 3])  # TypeError
# foo(y=2, *[1, 3])  # TypeError
foo(z=3, *[1, 2])  # (1, 2, 3)  # No error, but confusing!

rules.name: empty-method-without-abstract-decorator
rules.description: {name} is an empty method in an abstract base class, but has no abstract decorator

rules.name: no-explicit-stacklevel
rules.description: No explicit stacklevel keyword argument found
rules.exampleBad:
import warnings

warnings.warn("This is a warning")

rules.name: except-with-empty-tuple
rules.description: Using except* (): with an empty tuple does not catch anything; add exceptions to handle

rules.name: except-with-non-exception-classes
rules.description: except* handlers should only be exception classes or tuples of exception classes

rules.name: reuse-of-groupby-generator
rules.description: Using the generator returned from itertools.groupby() more than once will do nothing on the second usage

rules.name: unintentional-type-annotation
rules.description: Possible unintentional type annotation (using : ). Did you mean to assign (using = )?

rules.name: duplicate-value
rules.description: Sets should not contain duplicate item {value}

rules.name: re-sub-positional-args
rules.description: {method} should pass {param_name} and flags as keyword arguments to avoid confusion due to unintuitive argument positions
rules.exampleBad:
import re

re.split("pattern", "replacement", 1)
rules.exampleGood:
import re

re.split("pattern", "replacement", maxsplit=1)

rules.name: static-key-dict-comprehension
rules.description: Dictionary comprehension uses static key: {key}
rules.exampleBad:
data = ["some", "Data"]
{"key": value.upper() for value in data}
rules.exampleGood:
data = ["some", "Data"]
{value: value.upper() for value in data}

rules.name: mutable-contextvar-default
rules.description: Do not use mutable data structures for ContextVar defaults
rules.exampleBad:
from contextvars import ContextVar


cv: ContextVar[list] = ContextVar("cv", default=[])
rules.exampleGood:
from contextvars import ContextVar


cv: ContextVar[list | None] = ContextVar("cv", default=None)

...

if cv.get() is None:
    cv.set([])

rules.name: del-attr-with-constant
rules.description: Do not call delattr with a constant attribute value. It is not any safer than normal property deletion.
rules.exampleBad: delattr(obj, "foo")
rules.exampleGood: del obj.foo

rules.name: return-in-generator
rules.description: Using yield and return {value} in a generator function can lead to confusing behavior
rules.exampleGood:
>>> list(get_file_paths(file_types=["cfg", "toml"]))
[PosixPath('setup.cfg'), PosixPath('pyproject.toml')]
>>> list(get_file_paths())
[]

rules.name: class-as-data-structure
rules.description: Class could be dataclass or namedtuple

rules.name: raise-without-from-inside-except
rules.description: Within an except* clause, raise exceptions with raise ... from err or raise ... from None to distinguish them from errors in exception handling

rules.name: zip-without-explicit-strict
rules.description: zip() without an explicit strict= parameter
rules.exampleBad: zip(a, b)
rules.exampleGood: zip(a, b, strict=True)

rules.name: loop-iterator-mutation
rules.description: Mutation to loop iterable {name} during iteration
rules.exampleBad:
items = [1, 2, 3]

for item in items:
    print(item)

    # Create an infinite loop by appending to the list.
    items.append(item)

rules.name: batched-without-explicit-strict
rules.description: itertools.batched() without an explicit strict parameter
rules.exampleBad:
import itertools

itertools.batched(iterable, n)
rules.exampleGood:
import itertools

itertools.batched(iterable, n, strict=True)

rules.name: map-without-explicit-strict
rules.description: map() without an explicit strict= parameter
rules.exampleBad: map(f, a, b)
rules.exampleGood: map(f, a, b, strict=True)

rules.name: blind-except
rules.description: Do not catch blind exception: {name}

rules.name: unnecessary-generator-list
rules.description: Unnecessary generator (rewrite using list() )
rules.exampleBad:
list(f(x) for x in foo)
list(x for x in foo)
list((x for x in foo))
rules.exampleGood:
[f(x) for x in foo]
list(foo)
list(foo)

rules.name: unnecessary-generator-set
rules.description: Unnecessary generator (rewrite using set() )
rules.exampleBad:
set(f(x) for x in foo)
set(x for x in foo)
set((x for x in foo))

rules.name: unnecessary-generator-dict
rules.description: Unnecessary generator (rewrite as a dict comprehension)
rules.exampleBad: dict((x, f(x)) for x in foo)
rules.exampleGood: {x: f(x) for x in foo}

rules.name: unnecessary-list-comprehension-set
rules.description: Unnecessary list comprehension (rewrite as a set comprehension)
rules.exampleBad: set([f(x) for x in foo])
rules.exampleGood: {f(x) for x in foo}

rules.name: unnecessary-list-comprehension-dict
rules.description: Unnecessary list comprehension (rewrite as a dict comprehension)
rules.exampleBad: dict([(x, f(x)) for x in foo])
rules.exampleGood: {x: f(x) for x in foo}

rules.name: unnecessary-literal-set
rules.description: Unnecessary {kind} literal (rewrite as a set literal)

rules.name: unnecessary-literal-dict
rules.description: Unnecessary {obj_type} literal (rewrite as a dict literal)

rules.name: unnecessary-collection-call
rules.description: Unnecessary {kind}() call (rewrite as a literal)
rules.exampleBad:
dict()
dict(a=1, b=2)
list()
tuple()

rules.name: unnecessary-literal-within-tuple-call
rules.description: Unnecessary list literal passed to tuple() (rewrite as a tuple literal)
rules.exampleBad:
tuple([1, 2])
tuple((1, 2))
tuple([x for x in range(10)])
rules.exampleGood:
(1, 2)
(1, 2)
tuple(x for x in range(10))

rules.name: unnecessary-literal-within-list-call
rules.description: Unnecessary list literal passed to list() (remove the outer call to list() )

rules.name: unnecessary-list-call
rules.description: Unnecessary list() call (remove the outer call to list() )
rules.exampleBad: list([f(x) for x in foo])
rules.exampleGood: [f(x) for x in foo]

rules.name: unnecessary-call-around-sorted
rules.description: Unnecessary {func}() call around sorted()
rules.exampleBad: reversed(sorted(iterable))
rules.exampleGood: sorted(iterable, reverse=True)

rules.name: unnecessary-double-cast-or-process
rules.description: Unnecessary {inner}() call within {outer}()
rules.exampleBad: list(tuple(iterable))
rules.exampleGood: list(iterable)

rules.name: unnecessary-subscript-reversal
rules.description: Unnecessary subscript reversal of iterable within {func}()
rules.exampleBad:
sorted(iterable[::-1])
set(iterable[::-1])
reversed(iterable[::-1])
rules.exampleGood:
sorted(iterable)
set(iterable)
iterable

rules.name: unnecessary-comprehension
rules.description: Unnecessary {kind} comprehension (rewrite using {kind}() )
rules.exampleGood:
dict(iterable)
list(iterable)
set(iterable)

rules.name: unnecessary-map
rules.description: Unnecessary map() usage (rewrite using a {object_type})
rules.exampleBad: map(lambda x: x + 1, iterable)
rules.exampleGood: (x + 1 for x in iterable)

rules.name: unnecessary-literal-within-dict-call
rules.description: Unnecessary dict {kind} passed to dict() (remove the outer call to dict() )
rules.exampleBad:
dict({})
dict({"a": 1})

rules.name: unnecessary-comprehension-in-call
rules.description: Unnecessary list comprehension
rules.exampleBad:
In [1]: %timeit all([i for i in range(1000)])
8.14 µs ± 25.4 ns per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 100,000 loops each)

In [2]: %timeit all(i for i in range(1000))
212 ns ± 0.892 ns per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 1,000,000 loops each)
rules.exampleGood:
any([x.id for x in bar])
all([x.id for x in bar])
sum([x.val for x in bar])
min([x.val for x in bar])
max([x.val for x in bar])

rules.name: unnecessary-dict-comprehension-for-iterable
rules.description: Unnecessary dict comprehension for iterable; use dict.fromkeys instead
rules.exampleBad:
{a: None for a in iterable}
{a: 1 for a in iterable}
rules.exampleGood:
dict.fromkeys(iterable)
dict.fromkeys(iterable, 1)

rules.name: undocumented-public-module
rules.description: Missing docstring in public module

class FasterThanLightError(ZeroDivisionError): ...


def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float: ...
rules.exampleGood:
"""Utility functions and classes for calculating speed.

This module provides:
- FasterThanLightError: exception when FTL speed is calculated;
- calculate_speed: calculate speed given distance and time.
"""


class FasterThanLightError(ZeroDivisionError): ...


def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float: ...

rules.name: undocumented-public-class
rules.description: Missing docstring in public class
rules.exampleGood:
class Player:
    """A player in the game.

    Attributes
    ----------
    name : str
        The name of the player.
    points : int
        The number of points the player has.

    Methods
    -------
    add_points(points: int) -> None
        Add points to the player's score.
    """

    def __init__(self, name: str, points: int = 0) -> None:
        self.name: str = name
        self.points: int = points

    def add_points(self, points: int) -> None:
        self.points += points

rules.name: undocumented-public-method
rules.description: Missing docstring in public method
rules.exampleGood:
class Cat(Animal):
    def greet(self, happy: bool = True):
        """Print a greeting from the cat.

        Parameters
        ----------
        happy : bool, optional
            Whether the cat is happy, is True by default.

        Raises
        ------
        ValueError
            If the cat is not happy.
        """
        if happy:
            print("Meow!")
        else:
            raise ValueError("Tried to greet an unhappy cat.")

rules.name: undocumented-public-function
rules.description: Missing docstring in public function
rules.exampleGood:
def calculate_speed(distance: float, time: float) -> float:
    """Calculate speed as distance divided by time.

    Parameters
    ----------
    distance : float
        Distance traveled.
    time : float
        Time spent traveling.

    Returns
    -------
    float
        Speed as distance divided by time.

    Raises
    ------
    FasterThanLightError
        If speed is greater than the speed of light.
    """
    try:
        return distance / time
    except ZeroDivisionError as exc:
        raise FasterThanLightError from exc

from typing import Annotated

rules.description: Using the global statement to update {name} is discouraged

var = 1

    var = 10
    print(var)


foo()
print(var)

def foo():
    var = 10
    print(var)

rules.name: subprocess-popen-preexec-fn

import subprocess

subprocess.run(["ls", "nonexistent"], check=True)  # Raises exception.

rules.name: unspecified-encoding
rules.description: {function_name} in text mode without explicit encoding argument

rules.exampleGood: open("file.txt")

rules.name: eq-without-hash

rules.name: useless-with-lock
rules.description: Threading lock directly created in with statement has no effect

import threading

counter = 0

    global counter

        counter += 1
rules.exampleGood:
import threading

counter = 0
lock = threading.Lock()


def increment():
    global counter

    with lock:
        counter += 1

rules.name: redefined-loop-name
rules.description: Outer {outer_kind} variable {name} overwritten by inner {inner_kind} target

for i in range(10):

    print(i)  # prints 9 every iteration

for i in range(10):
    for i in range(10):  # original value overwritten
        pass
    print(i)  # also prints 9 every iteration

with path1.open() as f:

        f = path2.open()
    print(f.readline())  # prints a line from path2

rules.name: bad-dunder-method-name

maximum = max(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)

def test_foo(mocker):

import pytest


def test_foo():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):

    # empty string is also an error
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=""):
        ...

import pytest


def test_foo():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="expected message"):
        ...

rules.name: pytest-raises-with-multiple-statements
rules.description: pytest.raises() block should contain a single simple statement

        func_to_test()  # not executed if `setup()` raises `MyError`
        assert foo()  # not executed

import pytest


def test_foo():
    setup()
    with pytest.raises(MyError):
        func_to_test()
    assert foo()

rules.name: typed-argument-default-in-stub
rules.description: Only simple default values allowed for typed arguments
rules.exampleBad: def foo(arg: list[int] = list(range(10_000))) -> None: ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(arg: list[int] = ...) -> None: ...

rules.name: pass-in-class-body
rules.description: Class body must not contain pass
rules.exampleGood:
class MyClass:
    x: int

rules.name: ellipsis-in-non-empty-class-body
rules.description: Non-empty class body must not contain ...
rules.exampleGood:
class Foo:
    value: int

rules.name: argument-default-in-stub
rules.description: Only simple default values allowed for arguments
rules.exampleBad: def foo(arg=bar()) -> None: ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(arg=...) -> None: ...

rules.name: assignment-default-in-stub
rules.description: Only simple default values allowed for assignments
rules.exampleBad: foo: str = bar()
rules.exampleGood: foo: str = ...

rules.name: duplicate-union-member
rules.description: Duplicate union member {}
rules.exampleBad: foo: str | str
rules.exampleGood: foo: str

rules.name: complex-assignment-in-stub
rules.description: Stubs should not contain assignments to attributes or multiple targets
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import TypeAlias

a = b = int

class Klass: ...

Klass.X: TypeAlias = int
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import TypeAlias

a: TypeAlias = int
b: TypeAlias = int

class Klass:
    X: TypeAlias = int

rules.name: unused-private-type-var
rules.description: Private {type_var_like_kind} {type_var_like_name} is never used
rules.exampleBad:
import typing
import typing_extensions

_T = typing.TypeVar("_T")
_Ts = typing_extensions.TypeVarTuple("_Ts")

rules.name: custom-type-var-for-self
rules.description: Use Self instead of custom TypeVar {}
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import TypeVar

_S = TypeVar("_S", bound="Foo")

class Foo:
    def __new__(cls: type[_S], *args: str, **kwargs: int) -> _S: ...
    def foo(self: _S, arg: bytes) -> _S: ...
    @classmethod
    def bar(cls: type[_S], arg: int) -> _S: ...

rules.name: quoted-annotation-in-stub
rules.description: Quoted annotations should not be included in stubs
rules.exampleBad: def function() -> "int": ...
rules.exampleGood: def function() -> int: ...

rules.name: docstring-in-stub
rules.description: Docstrings should not be included in stubs
rules.exampleGood: def func(param: int) -> str: ...

rules.name: collections-named-tuple
rules.description: Use typing.NamedTuple instead of collections.namedtuple
rules.exampleBad:
from collections import namedtuple

Person = namedtuple("Person", ["name", "age"])
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import NamedTuple

class Person(NamedTuple):
    name: str
    age: int

rules.name: unaliased-collections-abc-set-import
rules.description: Use from collections.abc import Set as AbstractSet to avoid confusion with the set builtin
rules.exampleBad: from collections.abc import Set
rules.exampleGood: from collections.abc import Set as AbstractSet

rules.name: type-alias-without-annotation
rules.description: Use {module}.TypeAlias for type alias, e.g., {name}: TypeAlias = {value}
rules.exampleBad: Vector = list[float]
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import TypeAlias

Vector: TypeAlias = list[float]

rules.name: str-or-repr-defined-in-stub
rules.description: Defining {name} in a stub is almost always redundant
rules.exampleBad:
class Foo:
    def __repr__(self) -> str: ...

rules.name: unnecessary-literal-union
rules.description: Multiple literal members in a union. Use a single literal, e.g. Literal[{}]
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Literal

field: Literal[1] | Literal[2] | str
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Literal

field: Literal[1, 2] | str

rules.name: any-eq-ne-annotation
rules.description: Prefer object to Any for the second parameter to {method_name}
rules.exampleGood:
class Foo:
    def __eq__(self, obj: object) -> bool: ...

rules.name: legacy-type-comment
rules.description: Don't use type comments
rules.exampleBad: x = 1  # type: int
rules.exampleGood: x: int = 1

rules.name: non-self-return-type
rules.description: __new__ methods usually return self at runtime

rules.name: unassigned-special-variable-in-stub
rules.description: {name} in a stub file must have a value, as it has the same semantics as {name} at runtime
rules.exampleBad: __all__: list[str]
rules.exampleGood: __all__: list[str] = ["foo", "bar"]

rules.name: bad-exit-annotation
rules.description: Star-args in {method_name} should be annotated with object

rules.name: redundant-numeric-union
rules.description: Use {supertype} instead of {subtype} | {supertype}
rules.exampleBad: def foo(x: float | int | str) -> None: ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(x: float | str) -> None: ...

rules.name: snake-case-type-alias
rules.description: Type alias {name} should be CamelCase
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import TypeAlias

type_alias_name: TypeAlias = int
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import TypeAlias

TypeAliasName: TypeAlias = int

rules.name: t-suffixed-type-alias
rules.description: Private type alias {name} should not be suffixed with T (the T suffix implies that an object is a TypeVar )
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import TypeAlias

_MyTypeT: TypeAlias = int
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import TypeAlias

_MyType: TypeAlias = int

rules.name: future-annotations-in-stub
rules.description: from __future__ import annotations has no effect in stub files, since type checkers automatically treat stubs as having those semantics

rules.name: iter-method-return-iterable
rules.description: __aiter__ methods should return an AsyncIterator , not an AsyncIterable
rules.exampleBad:
>>> import collections.abc
>>> x = [42]
>>> isinstance(x, collections.abc.Iterable)
True
>>> isinstance(x, collections.abc.Iterator)
False
>>> next(x)
Traceback (most recent call last):
 File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: 'list' object is not an iterator
>>> y = iter(x)
>>> isinstance(y, collections.abc.Iterable)
True
>>> isinstance(y, collections.abc.Iterator)
True
>>> next(y)
42

rules.name: unused-private-protocol
rules.description: Private protocol {name} is never used
rules.exampleGood:
import typing

class _PrivateProtocol(typing.Protocol):
    foo: int

def func(arg: _PrivateProtocol) -> None: ...

rules.name: unused-private-type-alias
rules.description: Private TypeAlias {name} is never used
rules.exampleBad:
import typing

_UnusedTypeAlias: typing.TypeAlias = int
rules.exampleGood:
import typing

_UsedTypeAlias: typing.TypeAlias = int

def func(arg: _UsedTypeAlias) -> _UsedTypeAlias: ...

rules.name: stub-body-multiple-statements
rules.description: Function body must contain exactly one statement
rules.exampleBad:
def function():
    x = 1
    y = 2
    return x + y
rules.exampleGood: def function(): ...

rules.name: unused-private-typed-dict
rules.description: Private TypedDict {name} is never used
rules.exampleGood:
import typing

class _UsedPrivateTypedDict(typing.TypedDict):
    foo: set[str]

def func(arg: _UsedPrivateTypedDict) -> _UsedPrivateTypedDict: ...

rules.name: no-return-argument-annotation-in-stub
rules.description: Prefer {module}.Never over NoReturn for argument annotations
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import NoReturn

def foo(x: NoReturn): ...
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Never

def foo(x: Never): ...

rules.name: redundant-literal-union
rules.description: Literal[{literal}] is redundant in a union with {builtin_type}
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Literal

x: Literal["A", b"B"] | str
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Literal

x: Literal[b"B"] | str

rules.name: unannotated-assignment-in-stub
rules.description: Need type annotation for {name}

rules.name: string-or-bytes-too-long
rules.description: String and bytes literals longer than 50 characters are not permitted
rules.exampleBad: def foo(arg: str = "51 character stringgggggggggggggggggggggggggggggggg") -> None: ...
rules.exampleGood: def foo(arg: str = ...) -> None: ...

rules.name: numeric-literal-too-long
rules.description: Numeric literals with a string representation longer than ten characters are not permitted
rules.exampleGood: def foo(arg: int = ...) -> None: ...

rules.name: unnecessary-type-union
rules.description: Multiple type members in a union. Combine them into one, e.g., type[{union_str}] .
rules.exampleBad: field: type[int] | type[float] | str
rules.exampleGood: field: type[int | float] | str

rules.name: unsupported-method-call-on-all
rules.description: Calling .{name}() on __all__ may not be supported by all type checkers (use += instead)

rules.name: byte-string-usage
rules.description: Do not use {origin}.ByteString , which has unclear semantics and is deprecated
rules.exampleBad: from typing import ByteString
rules.exampleGood: from collections.abc import Buffer

rules.name: generator-return-from-iter-method
rules.description: Use {return_type} as the return value for simple {method} methods

rules.name: generic-not-last-base-class
rules.description: Generic[] should always be the last base class
rules.exampleBad:
from collections.abc import Container, Iterable, Sized
from typing import Generic, TypeVar


T = TypeVar("T")
K = TypeVar("K")
V = TypeVar("V")


class LinkedList(Generic[T], Sized):
    def push(self, item: T) -> None:
        self._items.append(item)


class MyMapping(
    Generic[K, V],
    Iterable[tuple[K, V]],
    Container[tuple[K, V]],
):
    ...
rules.exampleGood:
from collections.abc import Container, Iterable, Sized
from typing import Generic, TypeVar


T = TypeVar("T")
K = TypeVar("K")
V = TypeVar("V")


class LinkedList(Sized, Generic[T]):
    def push(self, item: T) -> None:
        self._items.append(item)


class MyMapping(
    Iterable[tuple[K, V]],
    Container[tuple[K, V]],
    Generic[K, V],
):
    ...

rules.name: redundant-none-literal
rules.description: Use None rather than Literal[None]
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Literal

Literal[None]
Literal[1, 2, 3, "foo", 5, None]
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Literal

None
Literal[1, 2, 3, "foo", 5] | None

rules.name: duplicate-literal-member
rules.description: Duplicate literal member {}
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Literal

foo: Literal["a", "b", "a"]
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Literal

foo: Literal["a", "b"]

rules.name: pep484-style-positional-only-parameter
rules.description: Use PEP 570 syntax for positional-only parameters
rules.exampleBad: def foo(__x: int) -> None: ...

rules.name: redundant-final-literal
rules.description: Final[Literal[{literal}]] can be replaced with a bare Final
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Final, Literal

x: Final[Literal[42]]
y: Final[Literal[42]] = 42
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Final, Literal

x: Final = 42
y: Final = 42

rules.name: bad-version-info-order
rules.description: Put branches for newer Python versions first when branching on sys.version_info comparisons

rules.name: bad-quotes-inline-string
rules.description: Single quotes found but double quotes preferred
rules.exampleBad: foo = 'bar'
rules.exampleGood: foo = "bar"

rules.name: bad-quotes-multiline-string
rules.description: Single quote multiline found but double quotes preferred
rules.exampleBad:
foo = '''
bar
'''
rules.exampleGood:
foo = """
bar
"""

rules.name: bad-quotes-docstring
rules.description: Single quote docstring found but double quotes preferred

rules.name: avoidable-escaped-quote
rules.description: Change outer quotes to avoid escaping inner quotes
rules.exampleBad: foo = "bar\"s"
rules.exampleGood: foo = 'bar"s'

rules.name: unnecessary-escaped-quote
rules.description: Unnecessary escape on inner quote character
rules.exampleBad: foo = "bar\'s"
rules.exampleGood: foo = "bar's"

rules.name: unnecessary-return-none
rules.description: Do not explicitly return None in function if it is the only possible return value

rules.name: implicit-return-value
rules.description: Do not implicitly return None in function able to return non- None value

rules.name: implicit-return
rules.description: Missing explicit return at the end of function able to return non- None value

rules.name: unnecessary-assign
rules.description: Unnecessary assignment to {name} before return statement
rules.exampleGood:
def foo():
    return 1

rules.name: superfluous-else-return
rules.description: Unnecessary {branch} after return statement

rules.name: superfluous-else-raise
rules.description: Unnecessary {branch} after raise statement

rules.name: superfluous-else-continue
rules.description: Unnecessary {branch} after continue statement

rules.name: superfluous-else-break
rules.description: Unnecessary {branch} after break statement

rules.name: unnecessary-paren-on-raise-exception
rules.description: Unnecessary parentheses on raised exception
rules.exampleBad: raise TypeError()
rules.exampleGood: raise TypeError

rules.name: ambiguous-unicode-character-string
rules.description: String contains ambiguous {}. Did you mean {}?
rules.exampleBad: print("Ηello, world!")  # "Η" is the Greek eta (`U+0397`).
rules.exampleGood: print("Hello, world!")  # "H" is the Latin capital H (`U+0048`).

rules.name: ambiguous-unicode-character-docstring
rules.description: Docstring contains ambiguous {}. Did you mean {}?
rules.exampleBad: """A lovely docstring (with a `U+FF09` parenthesis）."""
rules.exampleGood: """A lovely docstring (with no strange parentheses)."""

rules.name: ambiguous-unicode-character-comment
rules.description: Comment contains ambiguous {}. Did you mean {}?
rules.exampleBad: foo()  # nоqa  # "о" is Cyrillic (`U+043E`)
rules.exampleGood: foo()  # noqa  # "o" is Latin (`U+006F`)

rules.name: collection-literal-concatenation
rules.description: Consider {expression} instead of concatenation

rules.name: asyncio-dangling-task
rules.description: Store a reference to the return value of {expr}.{method}
rules.exampleBad:
import asyncio

for i in range(10):
    # This creates a weak reference to the task, which may be garbage
    # collected at any time.
    asyncio.create_task(some_coro(param=i))
rules.exampleGood:
import asyncio

background_tasks = set()

for i in range(10):
    task = asyncio.create_task(some_coro(param=i))

    # Add task to the set. This creates a strong reference.
    background_tasks.add(task)

    # To prevent keeping references to finished tasks forever,
    # make each task remove its own reference from the set after
    # completion:
    task.add_done_callback(background_tasks.discard)

rules.name: zip-instead-of-pairwise
rules.description: Prefer itertools.pairwise() over zip() when iterating over successive pairs
rules.exampleBad:
letters = "ABCD"
zip(letters, letters[1:])  # ("A", "B"), ("B", "C"), ("C", "D")
rules.exampleGood:
from itertools import pairwise

letters = "ABCD"
pairwise(letters)  # ("A", "B"), ("B", "C"), ("C", "D")

rules.name: mutable-dataclass-default
rules.description: Do not use mutable default values for dataclass attributes

rules.name: function-call-in-dataclass-default-argument
rules.description: Do not perform function call {name} in dataclass defaults

rules.name: explicit-f-string-type-conversion
rules.description: Use explicit conversion flag
rules.exampleBad:
a = "some string"
f"{repr(a)}"
rules.exampleGood:
a = "some string"
f"{a!r}"

rules.name: mutable-class-default
rules.description: Mutable default value for class attribute

rules.name: implicit-optional
rules.description: PEP 484 prohibits implicit Optional
rules.exampleBad:
def foo(arg: int = None):
    pass

rules.name: unnecessary-iterable-allocation-for-first-element
rules.description: Prefer next({iterable}) over single element slice
rules.exampleBad:
head = list(x)[0]
head = [x * x for x in range(10)][0]
rules.exampleGood:
head = next(iter(x))
head = next(x * x for x in range(10))

rules.name: invalid-index-type
rules.description: Slice in indexed access to type {value_type} uses type {index_type} instead of an integer

rules.name: quadratic-list-summation
rules.description: Avoid quadratic list summation
rules.exampleGood:
import functools
import operator

lists = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]
joined = functools.reduce(operator.iadd, lists, [])

rules.name: assignment-in-assert
rules.description: Avoid assignment expressions in assert statements
rules.exampleBad:
assert (x := 0) == 0
print(x)
rules.exampleGood:
x = 0
assert x == 0
print(x)

rules.name: unnecessary-key-check
rules.description: Unnecessary key check before dictionary access
rules.exampleBad:
if "key" in dct and dct["key"]:
    ...
rules.exampleGood:
if dct.get("key"):
    ...

rules.name: never-union
rules.description: {never_like} | T is equivalent to T
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Never


def func() -> Never | int: ...
rules.exampleGood: def func() -> int: ...

rules.name: parenthesize-chained-operators
rules.description: Parenthesize a and b expressions when chaining and and or together, to make the precedence clear
rules.exampleBad:
a, b, c = 1, 0, 2
x = a or b and c

d, e, f = 0, 1, 2
y = d and e or f
rules.exampleGood:
a, b, c = 1, 0, 2
x = a or (b and c)

d, e, f = 0, 1, 2
y = (d and e) or f

rules.name: unsorted-dunder-all
rules.description: __all__ is not sorted
rules.exampleBad:
import sys

__all__ = [
    "b",
    "c",
    "a",
]

if sys.platform == "win32":
    __all__ += ["z", "y"]
rules.exampleGood:
import sys

__all__ = [
    "a",
    "b",
    "c",
]

if sys.platform == "win32":
    __all__ += ["y", "z"]

rules.name: unsorted-dunder-slots
rules.description: {}.__slots__ is not sorted
rules.exampleBad:
class Dog:
    __slots__ = "name", "breed"
rules.exampleGood:
class Dog:
    __slots__ = "breed", "name"

rules.name: mutable-fromkeys-value
rules.description: Do not pass mutable objects as values to dict.fromkeys
rules.exampleBad:
cities = dict.fromkeys(["UK", "Poland"], [])
cities["UK"].append("London")
cities["Poland"].append("Poznan")
print(cities)  # {'UK': ['London', 'Poznan'], 'Poland': ['London', 'Poznan']}

rules.name: default-factory-kwarg
rules.description: default_factory is a positional-only argument to defaultdict
rules.exampleBad:
defaultdict(default_factory=int)
defaultdict(default_factory=list)
rules.exampleGood:
defaultdict(int)
defaultdict(list)

rules.name: missing-f-string-syntax
rules.description: Possible f-string without an f prefix
rules.exampleBad:
name = "Sarah"
day_of_week = "Tuesday"
print("Hello {name}! It is {day_of_week} today!")
rules.exampleGood:
name = "Sarah"
day_of_week = "Tuesday"
print(f"Hello {name}! It is {day_of_week} today!")

rules.name: invalid-formatter-suppression-comment
rules.description: This suppression comment is invalid because {}

rules.name: unused-async
rules.description: Function {name} is declared async , but doesn't await or use async features.
rules.exampleBad:
async def foo():
    bar()
rules.exampleGood:
def foo():
    bar()

rules.name: assert-with-print-message
rules.description: print() call in assert statement is likely unintentional
rules.exampleBad: assert False, print("This is a message")
rules.exampleGood: assert False, "This is a message"

rules.name: incorrectly-parenthesized-tuple-in-subscript
rules.description: Use parentheses for tuples in subscripts
rules.exampleBad:
directions = {(0, 1): "North", (1, 0): "East", (0, -1): "South", (-1, 0): "West"}
directions[(0, 1)]
rules.exampleGood:
directions = {(0, 1): "North", (1, 0): "East", (0, -1): "South", (-1, 0): "West"}
directions[0, 1]

rules.name: decimal-from-float-literal
rules.description: Decimal() called with float literal argument
rules.exampleBad: num = Decimal(1.2345)
rules.exampleGood: num = Decimal("1.2345")

rules.name: post-init-default
rules.description: __post_init__ method with argument defaults

rules.name: useless-if-else
rules.description: Useless if - else condition
rules.exampleBad: foo = x if y else x
rules.exampleGood: foo = x

rules.name: none-not-at-end-of-union
rules.description: None not at the end of the type union.
rules.exampleBad: def func(arg: None | int): ...
rules.exampleGood: def func(arg: int | None): ...

rules.name: unnecessary-empty-iterable-within-deque-call
rules.description: Unnecessary empty iterable within a deque call
rules.exampleBad:
from collections import deque

queue = deque(set())
queue = deque([], 10)
rules.exampleGood:
from collections import deque

queue = deque()
queue = deque(maxlen=10)

rules.name: redundant-bool-literal
rules.description: Literal[True, False, ...] can be replaced with Literal[...] | bool
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Literal

x: Literal[True, False]
y: Literal[True, False, "hello", "world"]
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Literal

x: bool
y: Literal["hello", "world"] | bool

rules.name: unraw-re-pattern
rules.description: First argument to {call} is not raw string
rules.exampleBad:
# Literal is `1\n2`.
re.compile("1\n2")

# Literal is `1\\n2`, but the regex library will interpret `\\n` and will still match a newline
# character as before.
re.compile(r"1\n2")
rules.exampleGood: re.compile("foo\\bar")

rules.name: invalid-assert-message-literal-argument
rules.description: Non-string literal used as assert message
rules.exampleBad:
fruits = ["apples", "plums", "pears"]
fruits.filter(lambda fruit: fruit.startwith("p"))
assert len(fruits), 2  # True unless the list is empty
rules.exampleGood:
fruits = ["apples", "plums", "pears"]
fruits.filter(lambda fruit: fruit.startwith("p"))
assert len(fruits) == 2

rules.name: unnecessary-nested-literal
rules.description: Unnecessary nested Literal
rules.exampleBad:
ReadOnlyMode         = Literal["r", "r+"]
WriteAndTruncateMode = Literal["w", "w+", "wt", "w+t"]
WriteNoTruncateMode  = Literal["r+", "r+t"]
AppendMode           = Literal["a", "a+", "at", "a+t"]

AllModes = Literal[ReadOnlyMode, WriteAndTruncateMode,
                  WriteNoTruncateMode, AppendMode]
rules.exampleGood:
AllModes = Literal[Literal["r", "r+"], Literal["w", "w+", "wt", "w+t"],
                  Literal["r+", "r+t"], Literal["a", "a+", "at", "a+t"]]

rules.name: pytest-raises-ambiguous-pattern
rules.description: Pattern passed to match= contains metacharacters but is neither escaped nor raw
rules.exampleBad:
import pytest


with pytest.raises(Exception, match="A full sentence."):
    do_thing_that_raises()
rules.exampleGood:
import pytest


with pytest.raises(Exception, match=r"A full sentence."):
    do_thing_that_raises()

rules.name: implicit-class-var-in-dataclass
rules.description: Assignment without annotation found in dataclass body

rules.name: unnecessary-cast-to-int
rules.description: Value being cast to int is already an integer
rules.exampleBad:
int(len([]))
int(round(foo, None))
rules.exampleGood:
len([])
round(foo)

rules.name: needless-else
rules.description: Empty else clause
rules.exampleGood:
if foo:
    bar()

rules.name: map-int-version-parsing
rules.description: __version__ may contain non-integral-like elements
rules.exampleBad:
import matplotlib  # `__version__ == "3.9.1.post-1"` in our environment

# ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: 'post1'
tuple(map(int, matplotlib.__version__.split(".")))
rules.exampleGood: tuple(map(int, matplotlib.__version__.split(".")))

rules.name: dataclass-enum
rules.description: An enum class should not be decorated with @dataclass
rules.exampleGood:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum


@dataclass
class E(Enum): ...

rules.name: unnecessary-if
rules.description: Empty if statement
rules.exampleGood: import sys

rules.name: if-key-in-dict-del
rules.description: Use pop instead of key in dict followed by del dict[key]
rules.exampleBad:
if key in dictionary:
    del dictionary[key]
rules.exampleGood: dictionary.pop(key, None)

rules.name: used-dummy-variable
rules.description: Local dummy variable {} is accessed
rules.exampleBad:
def function():
    _variable = 3
    # important: avoid shadowing the builtin `id()` function!
    _id = 4
    return _variable + _id
rules.exampleGood:
def function():
    variable = 3
    # important: avoid shadowing the builtin `id()` function!
    id_ = 4
    return variable + id_

rules.name: class-with-mixed-type-vars
rules.description: Class with type parameter list inherits from Generic
rules.exampleBad:
from typing import Generic, TypeVar

U = TypeVar("U")

# TypeError: Cannot inherit from Generic[...] multiple times.
class C[T](Generic[U]): ...
rules.exampleGood:
from typing import Generic, ParamSpec, TypeVar, TypeVarTuple

U = TypeVar("U")
P = ParamSpec("P")
Ts = TypeVarTuple("Ts")


class C[T](Generic[U, P, *Ts]): ...

rules.name: indented-form-feed
rules.description: Indented form feed
rules.exampleBad: if foo():\n    \fbar()
rules.exampleGood: if foo():\n    bar()

rules.name: unnecessary-regular-expression
rules.description: Plain string pattern passed to re function
rules.exampleBad: re.sub("abc", "", s)
